public_apis_implemented: pub fn unpack_scale_min_k4_cpu(scales: &[u8; 12], j: usize) -> (u8, u8) (crates/axc-driver/tests/common_helpers.rs)
pub fn q4km_dequant_matvec_cpu(superblocks: &[u8], x: &[f32], n_sb: usize) -> f32 (crates/axc-driver/tests/common_helpers.rs)
pub fn generate_q4km_test_data(n_sb: usize, seed: u64) -> (Vec<u8>, Vec<f32>) (crates/axc-driver/tests/common_helpers.rs)
bench_additions: dispatch_q4km bench binary: crates/axc-driver/benches/dispatch_q4km.rs (separate OnceLock<GpuStateQ4KM> isolated from dispatch.rs).
bench_dispatch_q4km_128: n_sb=128, 32768 x-vector elements, BatchSize::PerIteration, pre-flight correctness check with 1e-3 ABS tolerance.
bench_dispatch_q4km_512: n_sb=512, 131072 x-vector elements, BatchSize::PerIteration, pre-flight correctness check with 1e-3 ABS tolerance.
AXC_Q4KM_BENCH_MAX_SUPERBLOCKS env var to cap superblock count (memory safety).
baselines.json gains two placeholder entries (median_ns=0) for dispatch_q4km_128 and dispatch_q4km_512.
design_doc_changes: DESIGN.md §3.1.11 added: Q4_K_M superblock layout (144 bytes/256 elements), get_scale_min_k4 bit-spread algorithm with CRITICAL note on q[j] (NOT q[j-4]) for m-high, dequant formula (no -8 offset), four-chunk iteration pattern, SPIR-V capabilities reused from M2.5, integration test AT-1301..AT-1331 reference, dispatch_gpu_q4km bench group reference.
DESIGN.md revision log entry added for M2.6.
key_bugs_fixed: Parse error: @complexity(O(n_superblocks * 256)) — Star token not valid in annotation arg; changed to @complexity(O(n)).
@equiv_fp_tol(1e-3) rejected: float literals not valid annotation args AND annotation unknown to HIR; removed from .axc file (pure consumer constraint — no compiler changes).
AT-1309 size check: SPIR-V is 7388 bytes, initial limit 4096 was too small; raised to 16384 (16 KB).
AT-1316 failure: used OpConvertUToF opcode 111; correct is 112 per spirv-headers (SpvOpConvertUToF = 112). Fixed OP_CONVERT_U_TO_F constant.
AT-1322 failure: for OpVariable, operands.first() is result_type_id, not result_id; result_id is operands.get(1). Fixed to inst.operands.get(1).copied().
Clippy duplicate_mod: common.rs loaded by multiple bench/test targets; fixed with #[allow(clippy::duplicate_mod)].
Clippy dead_code: common_helpers.rs helper functions unused in bench/test context; fixed with #[allow(dead_code)].
Clippy manual_is_multiple_of: bytes.len() % 4 == 0 changed to bytes.len().is_multiple_of(4).
Clippy this-operation-has-no-effect: (0xFF & 0x0F) constant fold; fixed to literal 0x0Fu8.
Clippy non_snake_case: at_1306_..._NOT_emitted and at_1308_..._NOT_emitted; fixed with #[allow(non_snake_case)].
implementation_notes: CRITICAL invariant preserved: in get_scale_min_k4 m-high clause (j>=4), the hi2 bits use scales[is0] (the original ggml q[j]), NOT scales[is0-4] (which would be q[j-4]). This matches ggml canonical form exactly.

Pure consumer: zero compiler changes. All SPIR-V capabilities (Int8=39, Int16=22, Float16=9, StorageBuffer8BitAccess=4448) and builtins (ptr_read_u8_zext, ptr_read_u16_zext, f16_bits_to_f32, f32_from_u32, band, lshr, bor, shl) are M2.5 infrastructure.
Dequant formula: y = d*sc*nibble - dmin*m. No -8 offset (unlike Q4_0). Nibbles are raw 4-bit values 0..15.

Four-chunk iteration: chunk in [0,4), is0=chunk*2, is1=is0+1; 32 lo-nibbles (sub-block is0) + 32 hi-nibbles (sub-block is1) per chunk. x-vector indexed as x[sb*256 + chunk*64 + l] (lo) and x[sb*256 + chunk*64 + 32 + l] (hi).
LCG (Knuth MMIX parameters) used for fixture generation to avoid rand crate dependency in test context.
AT-1331 is #[ignore]d: PhysicalStorageBuffer64 address model is NVIDIA-specific and not available on Lavapipe CI.

bdcfcef: feat(M2.6): add dispatch_q4km bench binary and register in Cargo.toml
63f3c7e: feat(M2.6): update postprocess registry, baselines placeholders, and DESIGN.md §3.1.11
1b055fa: fix(M2.6): apply clippy fixes to compile_q4km_dequant_matvec.rs
